snippetLines.lineText: Protocol message classes are almost always generated by the protocol
compiler.  These generated types subclass Message and implement the methods
"""Provides the list of all accessible Message attributes."""
* Abstract interface implemented by Protocol Message objects.

*

See also {@link MessageLite}, which defines most of the methods that typical users care about.
* {@link Message} adds methods that are not available in the "lite" runtime. The biggest added
* features are introspection and reflection; that is, getting descriptors for the message type and
public interface Message extends MessageLite, MessageOrBuilder {
// the internal library are allowed to create subclasses.
[[nodiscard]] inline const Message* DownCastMessage(const MessageLite* from) {
// Returns the number of extensions present in this message.
upb_Message* upb_Message_New(const upb_MiniTable* m, upb_Arena* a) {
void upb_Message_Freeze(upb_Message* msg, const upb_MiniTable* m) {
// This type is an implementation detail of the JSON parser.
static void Message_get(Message* intern, const upb_FieldDef* f, zval* rv) {
static bool Message_set(Message* intern, const upb_FieldDef* f, zval* val) {
static bool MessageEq(const upb_Message* m1, const upb_Message* m2,
msgdef;  // kept alive by self.class.descriptor reference.
const upb_Message* Message_Get(VALUE msg_rb, const upb_MessageDef** m) {
static PyObject* PyUpb_Message_New(PyObject* cls, PyObject* unused_args,
void upb_Message_LogNewMessage(const upb_MiniTable* m, const upb_Arena* arena) {
// Internal members of a upb_Message that track unknown fields and/or
// extensions. We can change this without breaking binary compatibility.
// Total number of entries set in aux_data
// Returns true if the message or any of its parents is frozen.

// Message with field names in camel case that conflict with accessors in the

bool upb_Message_SetMapEntry(upb_Map* map, const upb_MiniTableField* f,
UPB_API_INLINE void upb_Message_Clear(upb_Message* msg, const upb_MiniTable* m);
T GetMessageBaseField(upb_Message* msg, const upb_MiniTableField* field,
static bool upb_Clone_MessageValue(void* value, upb_CType value_type,
bool upb_Message_DeepCopy(upb_Message* dst, const upb_Message* src,
UPB_API_INLINE bool upb_Message_IsFrozen(const struct upb_Message* msg) {
/// - `mini_table` and `arena` must be valid to deref
pub fn upb_Message_New(mini_table: RawMiniTable, arena: RawArena) -> Option<RawMessage>;
/// - `mini_table` must be the MiniTable associated with `m`
pub fn upb_Message_Clear(m: RawMessage, mini_table: RawMiniTable);
// single upb_Message* at offset 0 of the upb_MessageValue.
upb_Message* dst, const upb_Message* src, const upb_MiniTable* mt,
/** Message couldn't be serialized because it is missing required fields. */

// MessageSet was the implementation of extensions for proto1.
// When proto2 was introduced, extensions were implemented as a first-class
// Google products with MessageSet-bearing messages to open-source environments.
// Same as upb_Message_NextExtension but iterates in reverse wire order
// Returns the minitable with the given field number, or NULL on failure.

static bool upb_Message_DoConvert(upb_Converter* c, upb_Message* dst,
bool upb_Message_IsEmpty(const upb_Message* msg, const upb_MiniTable* m) {
bool upb_Message_IsEqual(const upb_Message* msg1, const upb_Message* msg2,
// A depth_limit of zero means to just use the upb default depth limit.
// NULL, in which case the function tests whether the given key is present.
UPB_API bool upb_Map_Get(const upb_Map* map, upb_MessageValue key,
// This function is only legal to call for maps that contain messages.
bool upb_Map_Get(const upb_Map* map, upb_MessageValue key,
struct upb_Message* upb_Map_GetMutable(upb_Map* map, upb_MessageValue key) {

* Messages are created and mutated using generated code, and then
* accessed through reflective APIs exposed through mini table accessors.
